race.name: America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride
race.tagline: 72 miles around Lake Tahoe at 6,300 feet. Emerald waters, Sierra Nevada granite, and one of the most audacious event names in cycling — except it might actually be true.

vitals.registration: Online via Blood Cancer United (formerly LLS). Ride-only $150. Impact option $500 fundraising minimum. Team In Training $1,600 fundraising commitment.
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple well-stocked rest stops with water, food, and on-course support. Lunch included at finish.
vitals.cutoff_time: Generous time limit. SAG support available for all distances.
route_options: 100 miles (full loop plus out-and-back to Truckee)

climate.primary: Early June Sierra Nevada — cool mornings, warm afternoons, high-altitude sun
climate.description: Early June at Lake Tahoe delivers some of the best cycling weather in the Sierra Nevada. Morning temperatures of 40-50F at the start warm to 60-75F by midday. The air is dry and clear. The lake itself moderates temperatures along the shore, but exposed climbs to Spooner Summit (7,100 ft) can bring wind and cooler conditions. UV intensity at 6,300+ feet is significantly higher than at sea level — sunburn is a real risk even on cool days.
challenges: Cold morning start at 6,200+ ft — arm warmers and vest essential for early miles
High-altitude UV exposure — sunscreen is mandatory, not optional
Dry mountain air accelerates dehydration despite moderate temperatures
Occasional afternoon wind on exposed lakeshore sections
Late-season snow in heavy winters can affect road conditions (rare but not unheard of)

terrain.surface: Paved state highways (US-50, CA-89, CA-28, NV-28) circling Lake Tahoe through both Nevada and California. The route follows the lakeshore clockwise with two significant climbs — Emerald Bay (800 ft) and Spooner Summit (1,000 ft) — plus rolling terrain throughout. Road quality varies from excellent to rough in patches, particularly on the narrow Emerald Bay section.
features: Clockwise circumnavigation of Lake Tahoe — one of the world's most beautiful alpine lakes
Emerald Bay climb — 800 ft ascent with narrow, winding road and stunning overlook
Spooner Summit — 1,000 ft climb to 7,100 ft, the high point of the ride
Paved highways throughout — no technical demands beyond descending

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 60. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 59.

course_description.character: A clockwise circumnavigation of Lake Tahoe on paved highways through both Nevada and California. The route hugs the lakeshore for most of its 72 miles, delivering a constantly shifting panorama of emerald water, granite peaks, and dense pine forest. Two meaningful climbs — Emerald Bay and Spooner Summit — break up the rolling lakeshore terrain. The entire ride sits between 6,200 and 7,100 feet, giving even the flat sections a high-altitude character that sea-level riders will feel in their legs and lungs.
course_description.signature_challenge: Emerald Bay — the 800-foot climb on narrow road to one of the most dramatic overlooks in the Sierra Nevada. The scenery is the reward, and the narrow road with 2,500 riders is the test.

logistics.airport: Reno-Tahoe International Airport (RNO) — 60 miles, ~75 minutes to South Lake Tahoe. 130+ daily flights, 20+ nonstop destinations. Sacramento (SMF) is a 2-hour drive with broader flight options.
logistics.lodging_strategy: South Shore Stateline has casino hotels (Hard Rock/Golden Nugget, Harrah's, Harvey's) within walking distance of the start. Book early — June is peak season and the event fills nearby hotels. Budget $150-250/night for hotels, $200-400 for vacation rentals. North Shore lodging is available but requires driving to the start.
logistics.food: South Lake Tahoe has abundant dining options — brewpubs, California cuisine, and casino buffets. Pre-ride carb loading at any of the Italian restaurants on Lake Tahoe Blvd. Post-ride lunch is included with registration at the finish celebration.

history.origin_story: America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride launched in 1991, created by Bike The West — the Tahoe-area event company founded by Curtis Fong that had been organizing cycling events since 1984. The concept was simple: ride around Lake Tahoe and let the scenery do the talking. Over three decades, Fong built the event into one of the West's most beloved charity rides, partnering with the Leukemia & Lymphoma Society (now Blood Cancer United) to raise millions for blood cancer research. After 30+ years of stewardship, Bike The West transitioned event production to Blood Cancer United, which continues the tradition of pairing world-class scenery with meaningful fundraising.
notable_moments: 1991: First edition organized by Bike The West under Curtis Fong's direction
Multi-decade partnership with Leukemia & Lymphoma Society raises millions for blood cancer research
Curtis Fong's 30+ year stewardship establishes the event as a western US cycling institution
Event transitions from Bike The West to Blood Cancer United (formerly LLS) for continued growth
Consistently draws 2,500+ riders to circumnavigate one of the world's most beautiful lakes
history.reputation: The charity century with the best scenery in America. Where the fundraising has meaning and the views are worth more than the medal.

biased_opinion.summary: America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride has the most audacious name in cycling — and the scenery to back it up. Circumnavigating Lake Tahoe at 6,300 feet delivers 72 miles of emerald water, granite peaks, and pine-scented air that would make a European alpine pass jealous. The climbing is moderate. The competition is nonexistent. The charity mission adds emotional weight. None of that matters once you're riding the west shore with Tahoe glittering below you and the Sierra Nevada rising into a cloudless June sky. The name isn't marketing. It might actually be true.
strengths: Lake Tahoe: one of the most beautiful bodies of water on Earth, and you ride around the entire thing
Altitude experience: the entire ride at 6,200-7,100 ft gives it a high-mountain feel without extreme climbing
Community and cause: charity partnership with Blood Cancer United gives the ride emotional meaning beyond recreation
Accessibility: three distance options (35, 72, 100 miles) welcome riders from beginners to century veterans
weaknesses: Not a race: zero competition, no timing, no podium. If you need to pin a number and go fast, look elsewhere.
Lake Tahoe prices: this is a resort destination. Lodging and food are tourist-priced, especially in June.
Moderate terrain: ~4,000 ft of climbing is enough to work but won't satisfy dedicated climbers
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The ride you do for the scenery, not the suffering. 72 miles around one of the world's great lakes, at altitude, in June. The name is earned.

final_verdict.one_liner: 72 miles around Lake Tahoe at 6,300 feet. The name is audacious. The scenery makes it true.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want one of America's most scenic cycling experiences. Yes if you want a well-supported charity ride with community and meaning. Skip if you need competition, serious climbing, or technical terrain.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want Lake Tahoe with competition: try Tour de Tahoe (Bike Big Blue) in September. If you want a scenic US century with more climbing: try Death Ride or Levi's GranFondo. If you want a massive US charity century: try El Tour de Tucson.

videos.title: Cycling around Lake Tahoe for the FIRST TIME
videos.description:
My first time riding around lake Tahoe was AMAZING! I had Stefano be my guide and show me around this gigantic, and extremely beautiful lake. I brought my family to get away for the first time since the quarantine has lifted. So I talk at the end about my issues with finding balance between bikes and family when traveling. 

Cycling around lake Tahoe and especially cycling around Emerald Bay is must. But I'll be honest, the mountain bike trails were where it was at. I got to ride MTB twice and if I had to bring just one bike, it would be the MTB. The lake loop is not very challenging though, so it's a great ride for any skill level. Cycling in Lake Tahoe is best around June to October during a weekday if thats possible. And going clockwise is the perfed direction. 

#cycling #laketahoe #ridebikes

videos.title: America’s Secret Cycling Paradise - Hank’s Perfect Ride In Fayetteville
videos.description:
Fayetteville Arkansas isn't a place many of you would have thought about taking a bike holiday, but Hank is here to show you exactly why you're wrong! Perfect gravel, wide quiet roads and great coffee, will this be Hank's perfect cycling day?

In association with Fayetteville CX - https://www.cyclocrossfayettevillear2022.com
In association with Trek Bikes - https://www.trekbikes.com
In association with Rapha - https://www.rapha.cc

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest and fastest-growing online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling. 

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more. 

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring you the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

videos.title: Indoor Cycling Videos With Music | Virtual Bike Ride 1 Hour Portobello Road
videos.description:
Want to lose weight, perform better and live longer?
I'm a family physician sharing science-backed insights inside my free weekly newsletter: Join here 👉 https://dradrian.carrd.co
Indoor Cycling Videos With Music | Virtual Bike Ride
👉 Get 25% off the Running in Nature 2022 Calendar - limited time only:
https://www.lulu.com/en/us/shop/bradem-press/running-in-nature-2022-calendar/paperback/product-vn7e8e.html in amazing places at https://ko-fi.com/virtualrunningvideos
👉Join the channel https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCY95zbGK0vlAZ8Gk8OZqa1g/join

Enjoy this sunny, 1 hour cycling video on Portobello Road, near Dunedin, New Zealand. I have added metrics and music. I hope you enjoy. Let me know what you think in the comments. Thanks for watching!

Gear I use and recommend:
GPS Running Watch: https://geni.us/ZKUMIf
Action Camera: https://geni.us/LlehXxK
Running Shoes: https://geni.us/kilcLA

Welcome to the Virtual Running Videos Channel! On this channel you'll find treadmill running videos, indoor running videos, indoor running, virtual running videos, New Zealand scenery, and virtual run videos. I started this channel out of gratitude for the beautiful place I live and a desire to share that with people. You can experience running and with a virtual run in the beautiful outdoors from the convenience of your home. I hope you enjoy! Please leave a comment, I read every one.

Important Disclaimer: This content is strictly the opinion of myself and is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not intended to provide medical advice or to take the place of medical advice or treatment from a personal physician or health professional. Consult your doctor or qualified health professional regarding specific health questions and before beginning any nutrition, supplement, exercise or lifestyle program. Neither myself nor the publisher of this content takes responsibility for possible health consequences of any person or persons reading or following the information in this educational content. Please take care when exercising. Neither myself nor the publisher of this content take any responsibility for any accident, injury or any health problems which occur while watching this video. As with all exercise programs, when using our videos, you need to use common sense.  Check with your doctor before beginning any fitness program. There may be camera views, movements or positions that may make people feel more unsteady or unsettled. By performing any fitness exercises, you are performing them at your own risk.  This channel will not be responsible or liable for any injury or harm you sustain as a result of our fitness program, videos, or information contained. Thanks for your understanding.

Links may be affiliate links from which I receive a small commission, but where the price is the same for you. Affiliate links are a way I help offset maintenance and running costs of video production.

videos.title: Improve Your Cycling Endurance Without Riding More
videos.description:
You don’t need to go on long, steady bike rides to build endurance. Getting fitter so you can ride further and faster can be done even if you’re short on time. Manon and Hank explain some top time-efficient training techniques and strategies which allow you to increase your cycling stamina without spending hours and hours on the bike.

0:00 Intro
0:36 Do we need to ride for hours?
1:16 Polarised training
2:05 Measuring training intensity
2:33 Example training rides
3:37 Train indoors

Useful Links:
Visit the GCN Shop 👉 https://gcn.eu/shop

Do you have any top tips of your own for training when stretched for time? ⏱🚴Let us know in the comments below! 💬

If you liked this video, be sure to give it a thumbs up 👍 and share it with your friends!

Watch more on GCN...
📹 Get Stronger Legs On The Bike 👉 https://youtu.be/9VkEa34PsNg
📹 Watch our Editor’s Choice Playlist 👉 https://gcn.eu/editorschoice
📹 Or why not check out our Features Playlist 👉 ​​https://gcn.eu/gcnfeatures 

🎵 Music - licensed by Epidemic Sound 🎵
Blinding - Cushy
Discount Fruits - baegel
Le Chercheur - spring gang
Some Kind of Animal - Tigerblood Jewel
Suction Pop - SFX Producer
Wheat Paste - peerless
Whip Whoosh 4 - SFX Producer
Whip Whoosh 5 - SFX Producer
Who You Fighting? - Cushy
Youll Know Its Me - Tigerblood Jewel

#gcn #cycling #roadcycling #roadbike #bike #bikes #bikelife #cyclingfitness 

📸 Photos - © Velo Collection (TDW) / Getty Images & © Sprint Cycling Agency

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside Cycling

videos.transcript:
[Music]
building on our endurance is something
all a cyclists want to be able to do we
want to go further and faster than we
ever have done before yeah the winter
months is the perfect time try and build
on that endurance but is spending hours
and hours doing long steady rides the
only way to increase our endurance no no
it's not ain't nobody got time for that
so here are some alternative ways you
can build your endurance without
spending hours and hours on the
bikee
now it's fair to say that not many of us
will have the time to go out on the bike
and spend hours and hours out there
building our Base fitness but in the
past that's what we've been told we need
to do the winter when we were
professional athletes was basically 10
to 12 weeks in the winter slogging on
the bike doing long steady slow miles
mostly in the rain and that's what
winter was all focused on but now
thankfully times have changed and you
don't need to be doing that there are
other ways to build your endurance but I
mean you could go out and slog around
for hours and hours if you wanted to I'm
wet I'm wet
than first up let's chat about polarized
training now some of you might have
heard of polarized training or the 8020
Rule and others might be thinking what
in the world is it well it's a Training
Method that many athletes have been
using for decades this Training Method
divides the two low intensity training
and high intensity training and misses
out that middle ground which is known as
Tempo so basically you'll have 80% of
aerobic capacity and that means
basically you'll be going nice and easy
low intensity and then you'll be doing
20% at high intensity the idea here is
to build your aerobic capacity and then
when you do your 20% you'll be able to
go harder for longer potent po getting
more out of yourself that's the
idea as we said 80% of your training
should be done at a really low intensity
now if you haven't got a p met to heart
rate want Itor to go off it's the
intensity that you can pedal you can
still have a conversation with someone
next to you and take in all your
surroundings whereas if you pedling hard
you'd really be focusing on how your
body feels and you would not have any
time to look at the pr flowers in the
field now an example of this is doing an
hour ride with your mates nice and easy
taking in the views and the flowers and
then the intense stuff the hard stuff
the zone four the high intensity
20% this is when you got to concentrate
with your
riding and you got to just go nice and
hard
so an example of a high intensity
session could look something like 9
times 2 minutes at
120% of your FTP with around 3 minutes
rest in between you could do that
session or you can do something like 20
seconds on 40 seconds off time six and
then do that session five times or it
could be something really super simple
like finding your local climb and
smashing it up as many times as you
possibly
can another great thing about this way
of training is that not all of your
sessions need to be done Outdoors many
if not all can be done on the indoor
trainer meaning you will save load of
time and not faffing around with your
bike you can get on your bike or set up
on the trainer ready the night before to
make it even more time efficient now a
great example of not having spent hours
and hours on your bike is our very own
Oliver Bridgewood he recently completed
over 1,000 kilom in just 3 days and you
think to complete that distance you'd
have to be doing hours and hours of
training on the bike well you'd be wrong
because Ollie actually spent the
majority of his training indoors doing
an hour or two on the indoor trainer and
he only did one 7h hour ride before he
took on that 3-day epic so yes he did
not spend hours and hours on the bike no
and Ollie has admitted to being a fair
weather Rider and if there is any chance
of rain he will be on the indoor trainer
but don't go thinking that you have to
put your big girl pants and socks or big
boy pants and socks on and go out riding
in the horrible weather to improve your
endurance because you don't so there you
have it time efficient training is
possible and you can still improve that
endurance of yours yeah enjoy the winter
we look forward to seeing your results
let us know in the comment section below
if you enjoy this video and you know to
do hit that Thumbs Up Button because we
know you love it well we hope you do
they do

videos.title: Italy’s Ultimate Bike Ride - GCN’s Dolomite Epic
videos.description:
When you think about cycling, Italy is a country that always stands out. The racing history, scenery and iconic brands all make it an epic place to ride your bike. We went to the Dolomites, the ultimate cycling destination, for a 120km dream ride over four legendary mountain passes: the Pordoi, Giau, Falzarego and Fedaia. This is Italy: GCN’s perfect ride!

In association with @CampagnoloSrl 👉 https://gcn.eu/Campagnolo
In association with @colnagoTV 👉 https://gcn.eu/Colnago

This route and loads more are available on Komoot, the Giro's official mapping partner:
GCN'S Dolomite Epic Route 👉 https://gcn.eu/ItalyEpicRoute
All stages of the Giro d'Italia 👉 https://gcn.eu/KomootGiro

Watch the in-depth bike check over on @gcntech 👉 https://gcntech.co/ItalyBikeCheck

Useful Links:
Adventure Documentaries, Exclusive Shows & Live Racing on GCN+: https://gcn.eu/plus
Download the GCN App for free: https://gcn.eu/app
Join us at the Global Bike Festival: https://gcn.eu/global-bike-festival 
Visit the GCN Shop: https://gcn.eu/procyclingguide
Join the GCN Club: https://www.gcnclub.com/

Which climbs would you choose to tackle on your perfect Italian ride? Let us know in the comments below! 💬

If you liked this video, be sure to give it a thumbs up 👍 and share it with your friends!

Watch more on GCN...
📹 The Legendary Gravel Race You Need To Know 👉 https://gcn.eu/Paris-Ancaster
📹 Watch our Editor’s Choice Playlist 👉 https://gcn.eu/editorschoice
📹 Or why not check out our Features Playlist 👉 ​​https://gcn.eu/gcnfeatures 

🎵 Music - licensed by Epidemic Sound 🎵

rider_intel.search_text: The provided transcripts do not contain information about America's Most Beautiful Bike Ride in Stateline, Nevada. The transcripts include content about Italian cycling in the Dolomites, a virtual running video with music, and general cycling training advice, but contain no rider intelligence or race-specific information about the 72-mile gravel race with 4,024 feet of elevation gain in Nevada.
